items.title: Child neglect being 'hushed up' in outback communities, says ex-minister

children.content: A review has begun into the family circumstances of Kumanjayi Little Baby leading up to her disappearance and death, which relatives hope will find answers.

gallery.altText: An Indigenous woman with grey curly hair wearing a black top sitting in a living room.

thumbnail.altText: An Indigenous woman with grey curly hair wearing a black top sitting in a living room.

items.title: Inpex seeks urgent orders to stop strike action in Darwin

children.content: Oil and gas giant Inpex has launched proceedings with the Fair Work Commission seeking urgent orders to stop strike action at its Ichthys LNG facility in Darwin.

image.altText: Inpex shipment

items.title: Mining set to restart at one of the NT's biggest gold reserves

children.content: After being dormant for nearly 30 years, the Mount Todd gold mine near Katherine is gearing up to restart production. While there are calls for local Aboriginal people to have the "first crack" at mining jobs, the operator is planning to engage a largely FIFO workforce.

image.altText: A miner inspects a large rock.

items.title: Police release body-worn footage of fugitive bikie's dramatic arrest

children.content: Northern Territory Police have released dramatic body-worn footage of the arrest of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham after the 44-year-old jumped a court dock last week, sparking a five-day manhunt.

image.altText: Nold_GrahamArrest_1006

articles.title: Mongols fugitive taken down by dog squad after fleeing traffic stop on foot

children.content: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

image.altText: A shirtless man in jeans with his face blurred is surrounded by three police officers who hold his arms

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: NT's only youth mental health ward found to be unsafe, FOI documents reveal

children.content: The Northern Territory's only youth mental health inpatient ward failed to meet 16 national safety standards, documents obtained under Freedom of Information laws have revealed.

image.altText: a multi-storey hospital building with a sign saying pandemic clinic visible

items.title: First Indigenous SANFL player David Kantilla inducted into hall of fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

image.altText: Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

items.title: Iron ore company 'undermines' promises after failing to pay traditional owners

children.content: Nathan River Resources went into administration and owes about $360 million to creditors including the NT government and traditional owners.

articles.title: Miner Nathan River Resources collapses owing at least $300m

children.content: Iron ore mining company Nathan River Resources has gone into voluntary administration owing at least $300 million in debt, unpaid wages and royalties.

image.altText: A haul truck is being loaded with iron ore in the open-pit of Roper Bar mine in the NT.

gallery.altText: A digger is filling up a truck with iron ore in the NT.

thumbnail.altText: A digger is filling up a truck with iron ore in the NT.

items.title: Police charge trio with Darwin man's murder in alleged 'vicious attack'

children.content: Police have charged three men who allegedly beat 48-year-old Darwin man Martin Wiberg to death in a "vicious attack" at the Casuarina bus stop earlier this year with his murder.

image.altText: Man holds three kittens under his chin

items.title: Rider killed in Finke Desert Race crash identified as Matthew Bielefeld

children.content: A motorcyclist who died while competing in this weekend's Finke Desert Race outside Alice Springs has been identified as Matthew Bielefeld.

image.altText: Two photos of a brunette man smiling, he is alongside a motorbike in one image and standing with a blonde woman in the other.

items.title: Fisherman attacked after treading on crocodile in northern WA

children.content: Authorities have confirmed the 35-year-old man was fishing in waist deep water at Barred Creek, north of Broome, when he was bitten.

image.altText: Mangroves and creek with a blue esky on shore

items.title: Hong Kong authorities charge seven people over deadly 2025 fire

children.content: The seven people charged included directors and inspectors of a consultancy firm involved in the estate's renovation.

image.altText: Two people wrapped in blankets watching the fires at Wang Fuk Court.

items.title: The abortion debate explained

children.content: Since 2023, abortion has been decriminalised nationwide — and polling consistently shows that most Australians support that.
However, now, abortion access is being debated in three different parts of the country.
See what's behind the push to revisit abortion laws and what impact the debate may have.

image.altText: A woman with long hair speaks to camera against a blue background. Text overlaid reads "The Abortion Debate Explained."

gallery.altText: A woman with long hair speaks to camera against a blue background. Text overlaid reads "The Abortion Debate Explained."

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long hair speaks to camera against a blue background. Text overlaid reads "The Abortion Debate Explained."

items.title: Twelve dead, nine injured after late-night shooting near Johannesburg

children.content: Police launch a manhunt for more than 10 suspects who launched a late-night attack at the Jumpers informal settlement in the Johannesburg suburb of Cleveland.

image.altText: A black man in white forensic outfit walks between vans as crowd of people look on from distance behind police yellow tape

items.title: Military officers behind acid attack on Indonesian activist sentenced

children.content: Four military intelligence officers are sentenced to up to three years in jail, after they threw sulphuric acid over a human rights activist in a brazen attack in Central Jakarta.

image.altText: Four men dressed in military fatigues line up in front of a judge in Jakarta

items.title: When does the FIFA World Cup start and who are the favourites?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

articles.title: Schwarzer confident 'written off' Socceroos can stun American doubters

children.content: Maligned, underestimated and overlooked — and that is just the way we like it according to the Socceroos' most capped player.

image.altText: Two men on a panel show, in a stadium, holding microphones

articles.title: FIFA prioritising profit over fans at World Cup, ex-official says

children.content: A former FIFA official says the upcoming World Cup has been tainted by corporate greed as two US states investigate ticket pricing and practices.

image.altText: Wearing a soccer jersey, Miguel Medina sits by a soccer field

articles.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you

children.content: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

children.content: Lawyers for the state government have admitted police "battered" former Greens candidate Hannah Thomas at a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney last year.

items.title: 'When Neale spoke, people felt it': Daniher farewelled at MCG funeral

children.content: Family, friends, members of the AFL community and dignitaries attend the service to remember the AFL legend, MND campaigner and former Australia of the Year.

image.altText: A painting of a man between the Australian and Aboriginal flag above a coffin draped in the Australian flag and native flowers.

items.title: Shaggy dog tale as Bruce rescued after being blown out to sea in kayak

children.content: Bruce, a dog, has been rescued from the North Sea after drifting away in an inflatable kayak.

gallery.altText: A large shaggy black dog with grey flecks on a nest of blankets.

thumbnail.altText: A large shaggy black dog with grey flecks on a nest of blankets.

items.title: Hayley's husband escalated control during her pregnancy. Her story is not uncommon

children.content: Pregnancy is a time of greater autonomy and self-awareness for women. Because domestic violence is about power and control, a pregnancy can challenge that for a person who is using abuse.

image.altText: illustration of woman holding her pregnant belly new version

items.title: Finding and saving the world's biggest trees before they disappear

children.content: It took a decade for researchers to find the tallest tree in East Asia. Now they know where the 84.1-metre conifer is, but the future of its species and other tall trees is dire.

image.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

items.title: Why was Rolf Harris never tried for his crimes in Australia?

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: NDIA employee charged over alleged $5m fraud plot against NDIS

children.content: Supermarket giant Woolworths will begin consultations with potentially affected staff today after announcing plans to shift some operations offshore.

items.title: Woman who died after surgery should not have been discharged, inquest finds

children.content: A woman who died days after gastric bypass surgery likely would have survived if she had not been discharged, an inquest has found.

items.title: Why Bunnings moving a store 10km down the road has caused such a stir

items.title: The proposed NDIS cuts advocates worry could put women at risk

children.content: Planned cuts to NDIS community participation budgets could leave participants in unsafe environments, including increasing their risk of domestic violence, advocates have warned.

items.title: Is Australia sucking the joy out of childhood?

children.content: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: playtime.

items.title: The ACT budget papers are out. Here are the five key takeaways
